name: The 3-2-1 Trick
summary: Stop rambling by forcing your brain to speak in a clear, numbered structure.
usage: Best for impromptu questions or when you feel the urge to over-explain.
interventionHint: 💡 Stuck? Try the 1 Thing: What's the most important point about this?

name: The P.R.E.P Framework
summary: A gold standard for clear, structured opinions that project authority.
usage: Ideal for job interviews, business meetings, or structured arguments.
interventionHint: 💡 Silence? Give a 'Reason' why your main 'Point' matters.

name: The P.A.R.A Method
summary: Creates depth without length by turning facts into micro-stories.
usage: Use this to answer 'What do you do?' or share project updates concisely.
interventionHint: 💡 Keep going! What was the 'Result' of that 'Action'?

name: Match, Mirror, and Lead
summary: Build instant rapport by adapting your energy to your listener's level.
usage: Best for one-on-one networking or connecting with introverted audiences.
interventionHint: 💡 Match the Vibe: Adjust your energy to the room's current level.

author: Chris Anderson (TED)
name: The Throughline
summary: Connect every part of your speech to a single, powerful central idea.
steps: Connect every story to that idea
